summarization.a: The chart shows the number of Al atoms added per unit area (Al/nm²) over cycles for both Al<sub>2</sub>O<sub>3</sub> experiment and model, and SiO<sub>2</sub> experiment and model. The Al<sub>2</sub>O<sub>3</sub> experiment and model exhibit a more similar trend, while the SiO<sub>2</sub> experiment and model deviate more from each other.

a.question: Which model consistently overestimates values compared to the experiment?
a.answer: Al<sub>2</sub>O<sub>3</sub> model generally overestimates the predicted values of added Al atoms.

a.question: Which model consistently underestimates values compared to the experiment?
a.answer: SiO<sub>2</sub> model generally underestimates the predicted values of added Al atoms.

a.question: Does the SiO<sub>2</sub> model capture the initial drastic drop in the Al atoms added that occurred during the experiment?
Considering only experimental data, which surface shows the greater amount of the Al atoms per area deposited during the first 3 cycles?
a.answer: Considering only the experimental data from cycles 1–3, SiO₂ has the greater total Al atoms deposited per area. Even though its rate drops in cycles 2 and 3, the  initial deposition of 4.5 Al/nm² in Cycle 1 outweighs the steady but lower start of the Al₂O₃ surface. Summing those cycles gives SiO₂ = 4.5 + 2.4 + 2.7 = 9.6, while Al₂O₃ = 2.5 + 3.2 + 3.4 = 9.1.
